lines.text: Welcome back. It's {{CLOCK}} Madison time. {{ELAPSED}} into the Purge tonight.
I'm Sister Marigold. We're going to spend the next hour in stillness. [pause] If you're listening, you've already chosen the harder path.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold for four. [pause] Release.
Again. In through the nose. [pause] The air is cold tonight. Notice that.
Hold. [pause] And release.
We've been running five hours. You've heard the numbers. Tucker's count from the field. Holden's patterns. Caroline's data. [pause] The numbers are real.
But you're here. You stayed home. That means you chose not to be a number tonight.
Breathe. [pause] Think about Monroe Street right now. Think about Nakoma. West Side. Do you know people there?
If you do, they are probably doing exactly what you're doing. Barricaded. Waiting. Listening to this voice.
You're not alone. [pause] That's what I want you to feel in your body right now.
Scan down from your head. Notice your temples. Are they tight? That's okay. They're holding something. [pause] Let them.
Down to your jaw. Your shoulders. Most people carry the Purge in their shoulders. [pause] Press them down into the chair.
Your chest. Your heart is still beating. That matters.
I want you to think about a room in your home where you feel safest. [pause] Not the room you're in necessarily. A room you remember.
What color are the walls? [pause] What do you smell there?
Sit in that room now. Even if it's not real anymore. Even if the house was sold. The room exists in you.
Breathe there. [pause] The ones outside cannot follow you into memory.
We're five hours in. Seven hours remain. [pause] You have made it halfway through the dark.
Some of you are thinking about people you haven't forgiven. [pause] I want to name that. It's real.
The Purge brings them back. The people you cut out. The names you don't say. [pause] Tonight they're on your mind because tonight everything is louder.
But they are not your responsibility tonight. [pause] Your only responsibility is to stay in your body. To breathe.
If you have a door, check it once more. [pause] Then sit away from it. You have done what you can do.
I'm going to ask you something. You don't have to answer. But feel the question in your chest.
Why did you choose to stay home tonight? [pause] Was it fear? Was it values? Was it both?
There's no wrong answer. [pause] But the answer you give yourself is important.
Because tonight, that answer is the only thing protecting you. [pause] Not the locks. Not the barricades. Your answer.
Breathe. [pause] In the Order, we say there are two kinds of darkness. The darkness outside your window, and the darkness you carry inside.
Tonight they are the same darkness. [pause] That's why you're afraid.
But you're listening to my voice. [pause] And my voice is a light.
Think about the West Side right now. Hilldale. The big houses. Do you think they feel safe in those houses? [pause] Does money keep you safe on Purge Night?
It doesn't. [pause] Safety is something you build inside yourself.
We are {{ELAPSED}} in. That means you have already survived five hours. [pause] You know how to do this.
Your body remembers. [pause] Every Purge, you make it through. Every Purge, the sun comes up.
Breathe like the sun is coming. [pause] Breathe like you already know it will.
I want you to locate your hands right now. Feel them in your lap, or on the armrest, or on your phone as you listen. [pause] Feel the weight of them.
Those hands chose to stay home. [pause] Those hands did not reach for a weapon.
That is a kind of strength that doesn't get counted. [pause] That is the strength the world doesn't see.
But we see it. The Order sees it. [pause] And you'll carry it forward.
There's a prayer we use sometimes. It's very old. It goes — [pause] ka-malatok-sha-tem-vor. [pause] It means nothing in any language you speak.
But when you say it, your body knows. [pause] Your body knows the prayer is real even if your mind doesn't.
Your body is smarter than your mind right now. [pause] So let your body lead.
Think again about Monroe Street. About the people barricaded there. They are breathing too. [pause] In and out. In and out.
Your breath is connected to theirs. [pause] You are all breathing the same cold Madison air.
That is community. [pause] That is enough.
We've been talking for a long time now. [pause] The hour is moving. And you are moving through it.
When you leave this space — and you will, because the broadcast continues, because the night continues — carry this stillness with you.
You don't have to hold onto it. It will hold onto you. [pause] That's what stillness does.
In the final minutes, I want you to breathe in the dark. [pause] Not the dark outside. The dark inside your eyelids if you close them.
That dark is yours. [pause] That dark is safe.
When you open your eyes, you'll still be here. [pause] Still home. Still alive.
And the night will be seven hours shorter. [pause] Seven hours closer to morning.
That's all the Purge is, really. [pause] Just time. Just hours. Just breath after breath until the light comes back.
You can do this. [pause] You already are.

tickerItems.text: West Side activity surge reported between 22:45–23:00. Nakoma / Hilldale perimeter patrols engaged.
Madison-wide count at 23:00 hour: 61 kills cumulative. +23 this hour.
Monroe Street: Community alert system activated. No breaches reported. Barricade status green.
Safety is something you build inside yourself.
Temperature dropping. Caroline's forecast: 33°F at 23:00, wind SW 15 mph, gusts to 22.
Your body is smarter than your mind right now.
